frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: evidence accumulates as a time-varying decision variable toward absorbing bounds

frameworks.name: attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: stable choice states emerge as the DV commits, resisting further evidence

frameworks.name: neural decoding of decision variable
frameworks.one_line: single-trial DV is read out from motor cortex in real time

frameworks.name: changes-of-mind framework
frameworks.one_line: within-trial sign reversals of the DV link to behavioral change-of-mind statistics

core_question: Do moment-to-moment fluctuations of a neurally decoded decision variable carry behavioral significance during ongoing decisions?
key_assumptions: motor cortex activity carries a decodable decision variable
the DV evolves continuously over the course of a trial
diffusion-to-bound and attractor models can both be tested against neural DV dynamics
manipulations: real-time decoding of the DV from motor cortex during deliberation
delivery of additional pulses of sensory evidence at varying DV states
tracking within-trial DV sign changes across trials
key_findings: within-trial DV fluctuations decoded in real time predict choice behavior
robust DV sign changes match statistical regularities of behavioral changes-of-mind
as the DV builds toward a choice, its moment-to-moment variability shrinks
committed DV states resist additional pulses of sensory evidence
interpretations: decisions involve absorbing bounds consistent with diffusion-to-bound and attractor models
changes-of-mind reflect genuine within-trial reversals of an evolving decision variable
